title: Loader validating-boundary contract with BoundedCursor and DecodeBudget
purpose: PR #5 patched three specific loader DoS holes (ETF recursion, unbounded Vec::with_capacity, zlib bomb), but the fixes are ad-hoc — each new chunk decoder can reopen the same vulnerability class. This brief installs the structural contract: a BoundedCursor that makes infeasible pre-allocations unspellable, a DecodeBudget that uniformly caps recursion/nodes/bytes across all decoders, and a per-load atom budget. The goal is that adding a new decoder inherits the safety properties automatically.
task: Refactor the loader's decode layer to use a unified BoundedCursor (validates counts against remaining bytes), a DecodeBudget (threaded through all recursive decoders), and an ensure_count helper. Consolidate the existing ad-hoc MAX_ETF_DEPTH and MAX_TABLE_ENTRIES checks into the budget. Add a per-load atom budget. The existing etf.rs depth-bounding and decompress_bounded in chunks.rs are kept but wired through the budget. Scope: loader/decode/ only. Does not touch import resolution or the interpreter.

requirements.title: BoundedCursor wrapper
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a BoundedCursor that wraps the existing Cursor and validates all element counts against remaining payload bytes before any pre-allocation. WHEN a decoder calls bounded_cursor.ensure_count(count, min_element_size), THE SYSTEM SHALL return an error if count * min_element_size > remaining bytes or count > MAX_TABLE_ENTRIES.
acceptance: BoundedCursor struct wraps Cursor and delegates read_u8/u16/u32/i32/read_bytes to it
ensure_count(count: usize, min_elem_bytes: usize) rejects if count > MAX_TABLE_ENTRIES or count * min_elem_bytes > self.remaining()
All Vec::with_capacity calls in chunks.rs are preceded by ensure_count
A crafted chunk with count=0xFFFFFFFF and 12 bytes of payload is rejected before any allocation

requirements.title: DecodeBudget struct
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a DecodeBudget with three counters: remaining recursion depth, remaining decoded nodes, and remaining allocated bytes. WHEN any counter reaches zero, THE SYSTEM SHALL return a budget-exhausted error. THE SYSTEM SHALL thread the budget &mut through every recursive decoder.
acceptance: DecodeBudget struct: { depth_remaining: usize, nodes_remaining: usize, bytes_remaining: usize }
charge_node(&mut self) decrements nodes_remaining, returns Err if zero
charge_bytes(&mut self, n: usize) decrements bytes_remaining, returns Err if insufficient
descend(&mut self) decrements depth_remaining, returns Err if zero
ascend(&mut self) increments depth_remaining (restores after returning from recursion)
Default budget: depth=256, nodes=1_000_000, bytes=64MB (configurable via constructor)
Budget is created per load_module call, not shared across loads

requirements.title: Thread budget through ETF decoder
requirements.spec: WHEN decode_external_term is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL pass the DecodeBudget &mut and call budget.descend() at each recursive entry and budget.ascend() on return. THE SYSTEM SHALL call budget.charge_node() for each decoded term. THE SYSTEM SHALL replace the existing MAX_ETF_DEPTH depth parameter with the budget's depth counter.
acceptance: decode_external_term signature includes &mut DecodeBudget parameter
The existing depth: usize parameter is removed — depth tracking lives in the budget
Every recursive call (tuple elements, list elements, map pairs) descends/ascends the budget
Every decoded term calls budget.charge_node()
Existing etf.rs tests pass with the budget threaded through
A term with 1_000_001 nodes (e.g. a flat list of 1M elements) is rejected by node budget

requirements.title: Thread budget through chunk decoders
requirements.spec: WHEN decode_atom_chunk, decode_import_chunk, decode_export_chunk, decode_lambda_chunk, decode_line_chunk, and decode_literal_chunk are called, THE SYSTEM SHALL pass the DecodeBudget and call budget.charge_bytes() for pre-allocations and budget.charge_node() for each decoded entry.
acceptance: All decode_*_chunk functions accept &mut DecodeBudget
Pre-allocations charge the budget before allocating
A module with atom_count=100_000 and export_count=100_000 and literal_count=800_001 exceeds node budget and is rejected
load.rs's top-level load_module creates a fresh DecodeBudget and passes it to all decoders

requirements.title: Bounded zlib decompression via budget
requirements.spec: WHEN the LitT chunk is decompressed, THE SYSTEM SHALL cap the decompressor output at budget.bytes_remaining via take(limit) and charge the budget for the decompressed bytes. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT inflate the entire stream before checking size.
acceptance: decompress_bounded uses decoder.take(budget.bytes_remaining + 1) to cap output
After decompression, budget.charge_bytes(output.len()) is called
A zlib bomb (small compressed payload that expands to > budget bytes_remaining) is rejected before exhausting memory
Existing decompress_bounded logic is preserved but wired through the budget's byte counter

requirements.title: Per-load atom budget
requirements.spec: WHEN atoms are interned during module loading, THE SYSTEM SHALL track the count of newly interned atoms against a per-load budget (MAX_ATOMS_PER_MODULE). IF the budget is exceeded, THE SYSTEM SHALL reject the module load. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT count atoms that are already interned (they are free lookups).
acceptance: MAX_ATOMS_PER_MODULE constant defined (default: 65_536 — matches BEAM's atom chunk u16 count limit)
DecodeBudget gains atoms_remaining: usize field
decode_atom_chunk increments atom count only for newly interned atoms (intern returns new index, not existing)
A module with 65_537 unique atom names is rejected during decode
A module that re-uses existing atoms (e.g. ok, error, true) does not count them against the budget

requirements.title: Consolidate constants and remove ad-hoc checks
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL centralise all decode limits in the budget module (or decode/mod.rs). THE SYSTEM SHALL remove redundant ad-hoc depth/count checks that are now superseded by the budget.
acceptance: MAX_ETF_DEPTH, MAX_TABLE_ENTRIES, MAX_ATOMS_PER_MODULE, default node/byte budgets all defined in decode/budget.rs or decode/mod.rs
No bare magic numbers for decode limits remain in chunks.rs or etf.rs
The existing ad-hoc depth parameter in decode_external_term is removed (superseded by budget.descend/ascend)
Existing tests pass — the budget's defaults match or exceed the old ad-hoc limits

requirements.title: Adversarial input test suite
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL include tests that exercise each budget limit with crafted inputs.
acceptance: Test: deeply nested ETF (depth > MAX_ETF_DEPTH) rejected by budget
Test: wide flat structure (nodes > node budget) rejected
Test: huge declared count with tiny payload rejected by ensure_count before allocation
Test: zlib bomb rejected by byte budget before OOM
Test: module with excessive unique atoms rejected by atom budget
Test: legitimate modules with normal structure load successfully within budget
All existing loader tests pass

boundaries: SHALL NOT change the loader's public API beyond adding budget/cursor parameters to internal decode functions
SHALL NOT add runtime/interpreter checks — this brief is decode-time only
SHALL NOT implement ETF safe-mode (rejecting fun/external-ref/port tags in literals) — that is a follow-up
SHALL NOT touch import resolution or the Module struct
SHALL NOT remove the existing ad-hoc hardening until the budget-based replacements are verified
verification: cargo test --workspace passes with all existing tests plus new adversarial tests
grep for bare 'Vec::with_capacity' in chunks.rs — every instance preceded by ensure_count or budget.charge_bytes
grep for 'depth: usize' parameter in etf.rs decode_external_term — replaced by &mut DecodeBudget
No raw magic numbers for decode limits outside budget.rs/mod.rs
A crafted .beam with any single oversized dimension (deep, wide, inflated, atom-heavy) is rejected with a descriptive error
